question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator acting as receiver use a collateral token with an ERC777-style tokensReceived hook to reenter withdraw() during liquidate()'s safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) call, under the precondition that marketState[id].withdrawable was already incremented by repaidUnits before the collateral transfer executes (Midnight.sol line 675), draining existing lender loan-token funds before the safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) pull on line 717 completes, violating the invariant that withdrawable must never exceed actual loan-token balance held by the contract, causing scoped impact: direct loss of lender funds / protocol insolvency? Proof idea: deploy a collateral token whose transfer() fires onTokensReceived calling midnight.withdraw(market, repaidUnits, lender, lender); assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)) < midnight.withdrawable(id) after the outer liquidate() returns.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a collateral token whose tokensToSend hook fires during safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) in supplyCollateral() to reenter withdrawCollateral() before the tokens are actually received, under the precondition that position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] is already incremented and collateralBitmap is already set (Midnight.sol lines 533-537) before the transfer, allowing the reentrant withdrawCollateral() to pass isHealthy() with the phantom collateral and transfer out real tokens, violating the invariant that collateral[i] must not exceed actual collateral token balance held by the contract, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal / direct loss? Proof idea: fuzz assets in [1, type(uint128).max]; deploy hook token; assert collateral[id][borrower][index] > 0 after withdrawCollateral succeeds but IERC20(collateralToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)) decreased by assets without a matching deposit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a collateral token whose tokensToSend hook fires during safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) in withdrawCollateral() to reenter take() as a seller (increasing debt) after isHealthy() has already passed (Midnight.sol line 568), under the precondition that the position was at the health boundary (debt == maxDebt - epsilon), causing the final position to be unhealthy with no subsequent health check, violating the invariant that every post-withdrawCollateral position must satisfy isHealthy(), causing scoped impact: bad debt creation / healthy-account liquidation bypass? Proof idea: set up position at LLTV=0.86e18 with debt just below maxDebt; hook reenters take() as seller for 1 unit; assert isHealthy(market, id, borrower) == false after outer withdrawCollateral returns.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay] Can an unprivileged repayer use a loan token with a tokensReceived hook that fires when Midnight receives tokens during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) in repay() to reenter withdraw() and drain the withdrawable units that were incremented on line 509 before the outer transfer completes, under the precondition that another lender has credit >= units and withdrawable >= units, causing the protocol to send loan tokens it has not yet received, violating the invariant that sum(lender credits) <= withdrawable <= IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)), causing scoped impact: direct loss of lender funds / insolvency? Proof idea: deploy hook loan token; repayer calls repay(units=X); hook calls withdraw(X) for a lender; assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) < midnight.withdrawable(id) after both calls settle.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (fee leg)] Can an unprivileged taker use a loan token with a tokensReceived hook that fires when Midnight receives the settlement fee portion during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) on line 455 to reenter withdraw() before the sell callback and health check execute (lines 458-476), under the precondition that withdrawable > 0 from prior repayments, draining withdrawable while the seller's liquidation lock is still set, violating the invariant that the health check on line 476 must execute against the final post-take state, causing scoped impact: fund freeze or accounting corruption? Proof idea: fuzz units and withdrawable; hook calls withdraw(withdrawable); assert midnight.withdrawable(id) == 0 and seller health check still passes after outer take returns.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (seller payment leg)] Can an unprivileged taker use a loan token with a tokensReceived hook on the receiver (receiverIfTakerIsSeller or offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller) during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) on line 456 to reenter take() with a new sell offer before the sell callback fires on line 458, under the precondition that the seller has remaining credit to reduce, causing the sell callback to observe stale sellerCreditDecrease and sellerPendingFeeDecrease values computed before the reentrant take, violating the invariant that ISellCallback.onSell receives accurate post-take accounting deltas, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption / callback payer confusion? Proof idea: deploy hook receiver; outer take sells N units; hook reenters take selling M more units; assert onSell's sellerCreditDecrease matches actual credit change.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (fee-on-transfer loan token)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a market with a fee-on-transfer loan token such that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) on line 455 deposits only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets - fee) while claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by the full buyerAssets - sellerAssets on line 418, under the precondition that the fee claimer later calls claimSettlementFee() for the full credited amount, causing the protocol to send tokens it does not hold, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(midnight)) minus withdrawable and collateral obligations, causing scoped impact: insolvency / direct loss of lender funds? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate in [1, 1000] bps; assert claimableSettlementFee[token] > IERC20(token).balanceOf(midnight) after N takes.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay (fee-on-transfer loan token)] Can an unprivileged borrower repay using a fee-on-transfer loan token such that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) on line 520 deposits only (units - fee) while position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced by the full units on line 508 and marketState[id].withdrawable is incremented by the full units on line 509, under the precondition that lenders subsequently call withdraw() for the full credited amount, causing the protocol to send more loan tokens than it received, violating the invariant that sum of all withdrawable credits <= IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)), causing scoped impact: insolvency / direct loss of lender funds? Proof idea: fuzz units and fee rate; after repay, assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) < midnight.withdrawable(id).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral (fee-on-transfer collateral token)] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral using a fee-on-transfer collateral token such that safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) on line 545 deposits only (assets - fee) while position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] is set to oldCollateral + assets on line 533, under the precondition that the borrower then borrows against the inflated collateral value, causing the protocol to hold less collateral than the position's maxDebt calculation assumes, violating the invariant that collateral[i] <= IERC20(collateralParams[i].token).balanceOf(address(midnight)), causing scoped impact: bad debt creation / undercollateralized borrowing? Proof idea: fuzz assets and fee rate; after supplyCollateral + take(sell), assert isHealthy() uses phantom collateral and IERC20(collateralToken).balanceOf(midnight) < position.collateral[i].

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / liquidate (fee-on-transfer collateral token)] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger a liquidation where safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) on line 696 sends only (seizedAssets - fee) to the receiver while position[id][borrower].collateral[collateralIndex] is reduced by the full seizedAssets on line 670, under the precondition that the collateral token charges a fee on outbound transfers, causing the liquidator to receive less collateral than the repaidUnits they pay, violating the invariant that liquidator net value (seizedAssets * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lif / WAD) >= repaidUnits, causing scoped impact: liquidation unprofitability / liquidation failure? Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets and fee rate; assert receiver's collateral token balance increase < seizedAssets and repaidUnits > actual collateral value received.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged actor (or the fee claimer itself using a hook token) use a loan token with a tokensToSend hook during safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, amount) in claimContinuousFee() to reenter claimContinuousFee() again before the outer transfer completes, under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit was already decremented on line 318 but the transfer has not settled, observing the already-decremented state and attempting a second claim, violating the invariant that continuousFeeCredit + totalUnits + withdrawable decrements are atomic with the token transfer, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption / fund freeze? Proof idea: deploy hook loan token; feeClaimer calls claimContinuousFee(amount=X); hook calls claimContinuousFee(amount=Y); assert totalUnits and withdrawable are consistent with actual token balance.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer use a loan token with a tokensToSend hook during safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) in claimSettlementFee() to reenter claimSettlementFee() for a different token that shares the same hook contract, under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] was decremented on line 307 but the hook fires before the transfer settles, allowing the hook to observe the decremented state and call claimSettlementFee() for a second token whose claimableSettlementFee has not yet been decremented, violating the invariant that each token's claimableSettlementFee is claimed at most once per call, causing scoped impact: double-claim / direct loss? Proof idea: two tokens sharing a hook; assert sum of claimed amounts > sum of claimableSettlementFee before claims.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / flashLoan] Can an unprivileged flash loan caller use a token with a tokensToSend hook during safeTransfer(tokens[i], callback, assets[i]) on line 743 to reenter flashLoan() with the same token before the IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan callback fires, under the precondition that the protocol holds sufficient balance, causing the protocol to send the same token twice without receiving repayment for the first send, violating the invariant that IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(midnight)) after flashLoan >= IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(midnight)) before flashLoan, causing scoped impact: direct loss of protocol-held tokens? Proof idea: hook reenters flashLoan for same token; assert balance deficit after both outer and inner flashLoan complete.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take + multicall (fee-on-transfer)] Can an unprivileged taker use multicall to batch take() + withdraw() in a single transaction with a fee-on-transfer loan token, such that take() credits the seller with sellerAssets units via withdrawable but the actual token received by the protocol is (buyerAssets - sellerAssets - fee), and the subsequent withdraw() in the same multicall drains withdrawable before the deficit is observable, under the precondition that the protocol has prior loan token balance, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)) at all times, causing scoped impact: insolvency / fund drain? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate and units; multicall([take(...), withdraw(units, lender, lender)]); assert balance invariant after multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / liquidate (callback reentrancy into withdraw)] Can an unprivileged liquidator use the ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate callback (fired on line 699 after collateral is sent but before loan token is pulled on line 717) to reenter withdraw() and drain the withdrawable amount that was incremented by repaidUnits on line 675, under the precondition that a lender has credit >= repaidUnits and withdrawable >= repaidUnits, causing the protocol to send loan tokens it has not yet received from the liquidator, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)), causing scoped impact: direct loss of lender funds / insolvency? Proof idea: liquidate callback calls withdraw(repaidUnits, lender, attacker); assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) < midnight.withdrawable(id) after liquidate returns.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay (callback reentrancy into liquidate)] Can an unprivileged repayer use the IRepayCallback.onRepay callback (fired on line 516 after debt is reduced on line 508 but before loan token is pulled on line 520) to reenter liquidate() on the same borrower whose debt was just reduced, under the precondition that the borrower was unhealthy before repay and the partial debt reduction makes them appear healthy, causing the reentrant liquidate() to fail with NotLiquidatable() or to liquidate at incorrect health state, violating the invariant that liquidatability must be evaluated against the final settled debt, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure / accounting corruption? Proof idea: set borrower debt just above maxDebt; repay callback reenters liquidate; assert liquidate reverts with NotLiquidatable after partial debt reduction.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (IBuyCallback reentrancy into repay)] Can an unprivileged taker use the IBuyCallback.onBuy callback (fired on line 448 after position state is updated but before safeTransferFrom on line 455) to reenter repay() for the seller's debt, reducing the seller's debt to zero before the outer take's health check on line 476, under the precondition that the seller has debt > 0 and the taker controls the buyerCallback, causing the health check to pass trivially (debt=0 => isHealthy=true) even if the seller's collateral was insufficient, violating the invariant that the health check must reflect the state produced solely by the take operation, causing scoped impact: bad debt concealment / accounting corruption? Proof idea: buyer callback calls repay(seller_debt, seller, callback); assert seller.debt == 0 at health check and no bad debt was realized.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (ISellCallback reentrancy into withdrawCollateral)] Can an unprivileged maker (as seller) use the ISellCallback.onSell callback (fired on line 461 after the loan token transfers but before the liquidation lock is released on line 475) to reenter withdrawCollateral() and remove collateral while the liquidation lock prevents liquidation, under the precondition that the seller's position is at the health boundary after the take, causing the final health check on line 476 to fail with SellerIsLiquidatable() and revert the entire take, or if the health check passes, leaving the position undercollateralized, violating the invariant that the seller must be healthy after take, causing scoped impact: fund freeze (DoS on take) or bad debt? Proof idea: sell callback calls withdrawCollateral(all_collateral); assert take reverts with SellerIsLiquidatable.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (ISellCallback reentrancy into liquidate on seller)] Can an unprivileged liquidator acting as the sell callback contract attempt to liquidate the seller during the ISellCallback.onSell callback (lines 461-473) while the liquidation lock is set via tExchange on line 444, under the precondition that the seller is unhealthy after the take, verifying that liquidationLocked(id, seller) == true prevents the liquidation with NotLiquidatable(), and confirming the lock is correctly released on line 475 before the health check on line 476, violating the invariant that the liquidation lock must prevent all liquidations of the seller during take callbacks, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure if lock is bypassable? Proof idea: sell callback calls liquidate(seller); assert revert with NotLiquidatable; then assert liquidate succeeds after take reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (payer confusion with fee-on-transfer)] Can an unprivileged taker set takerCallback to a contract that returns CALLBACK_SUCCESS without paying, causing payer = takerCallback (line 422) to be the callback contract, and then use a fee-on-transfer loan token such that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) on line 455 pulls from the callback contract but only deposits (buyerAssets - sellerAssets - fee), while claimableSettlementFee is incremented by the full amount, under the precondition that the callback contract has approved Midnight, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] <= actual fee tokens received, causing scoped impact: fee accounting corruption / insolvency? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate; assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance contribution after take.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw (rebasing loan token)] Can an unprivileged actor exploit a rebasing loan token that increases all balances between a take() and a withdraw() call, such that marketState[id].withdrawable reflects the pre-rebase units but IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)) has grown, allowing a lender to withdraw more units than their credit entitles them to by calling withdraw() after a positive rebase, under the precondition that the rebase increases the protocol's balance without updating totalUnits or withdrawable, violating the invariant that each credit unit redeems exactly one loan token, causing scoped impact: direct loss (other lenders receive less) / accounting corruption? Proof idea: fuzz rebase factor; assert sum of all lender withdrawals > initial loan token deposits.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw (rebasing loan token negative rebase)] Can an unprivileged actor exploit a rebasing loan token that decreases all balances (negative rebase) between repay() and withdraw() calls, such that marketState[id].withdrawable reflects the pre-rebase units but IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)) has shrunk, causing withdraw() to revert or drain other users' funds when the protocol attempts to send units it no longer holds, under the precondition that the rebase reduces the protocol's balance below withdrawable, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(address(midnight)), causing scoped impact: fund freeze / insolvency? Proof idea: fuzz negative rebase factor; assert withdraw() reverts for valid lenders after rebase.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (ERC777 tokensToSend on payer during fee pull)] Can an unprivileged taker whose address is the payer (no buyerCallback set, offer.buy=false so payer=msg.sender on line 422) use an ERC777 loan token whose tokensToSend hook fires on the payer before the transfer executes during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) on line 455, allowing the payer to reenter take() with a different offer before the first take's fee transfer completes, under the precondition that the first take's state is fully committed (positions, consumed, claimableSettlementFee all updated), causing the second take to execute against already-updated state, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] must reflect all fills before any external call, causing scoped impact: offer overfill / accounting corruption? Proof idea: ERC777 tokensToSend reenters take with same group; assert consumed[maker][group] > offer.maxAssets after both takes.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / liquidate (bad debt + collateral hook cross-contamination)] Can an unprivileged liquidator trigger a liquidation where bad debt is realized (lossFactor updated, totalUnits reduced on lines 631-634) and then the collateral token's tokensToSend hook fires during safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) on line 696, reentering liquidate() on a different borrower in the same market, causing the second liquidation to compute maxDebt using the already-updated lossFactor from the first liquidation's bad debt realization, under the precondition that the second borrower's health depends on the market's lossFactor via updatePosition, violating the invariant that each liquidation's bad debt realization must be isolated, causing scoped impact: incorrect liquidation / accounting corruption? Proof idea: two borrowers; first liquidation realizes bad debt; hook reenters second liquidation; assert second borrower's effective credit after updatePosition is consistent.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (false-return loan token)] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a market with a loan token that returns false on transferFrom() (instead of reverting), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to revert with TransferFromReturnedFalse() on line 33 of SafeTransferLib.sol, under the precondition that the token returns false only for specific callers or amounts, causing take() to revert after state has been fully updated (positions, consumed, claimableSettlementFee all committed on lines 368-418) but before the transfer, violating the invariant that state updates must be atomic with token transfers (since Solidity reverts unwind state), causing scoped impact: fund freeze (DoS on take for specific conditions)? Proof idea: token returns false for amounts > threshold; assert take reverts and state is correctly reverted by EVM.
